name: nautilus-ir
description: Syntax highlighting, navigation, and gdb-based debugging for Nautilus IR (.nautilus) files emitted by the Nautilus tracing JIT compiler.

commands.title: Nautilus IR: Toggle SSA Definition Highlight
Nautilus IR: Debug Current File with gdb

nautilusIr.gdbPath.description: Path to the gdb executable used for debugging Nautilus IR files.

nautilusIr.gdbExtraArgs.description: Extra command-line arguments forwarded to gdb when launching.

nautilusIr.highlightDefinitions.description: Highlight all uses of an SSA value when the cursor is on its definition.

nautilusIr.showInlayTypes.description: Show inline summary information for blocks and operations.

nautilusIr.codeLens.description: Show CodeLens above blocks (predecessors / successors / argument count).

nautilusIr.lastHostProgram.description: Last-used C++ host executable for ad-hoc gdb debugging. Updated automatically by the 'Debug Current File with gdb' command.

program.description: Absolute path to the C++ host executable that loads and runs the Nautilus IR (e.g. an ExecutionTest binary or your own demo). Nautilus IR is never executed on its own — gdb debugs the host process and stops in JIT-compiled IR code via source-line debug info.

args.description: Command-line arguments forwarded to the host program.

irFile.description: Optional Nautilus IR file the host program will load. If set, the adapter will tell gdb to look for source files in this directory and pre-set a breakpoint on its first instruction (when stopAtIrEntry is true).

stopAtIrEntry.description: If true, set a one-shot breakpoint at the first instruction of irFile so execution halts as soon as control reaches the JIT-compiled IR.

stopOnEntry.description: Stop at main() of the host program before running.

gdbPath.description: Override the gdb executable used for this session.

gdbArgs.description: Additional command-line arguments passed to gdb itself.

sourceDirectories.description: Extra directories that gdb should search for source files (forwarded as `directory` commands). Useful when the IR file lives outside the host program's source tree.

cwd.description: Working directory for the gdb process.

env.description: Environment variables forwarded to the inferior.

trace.description: Log all DAP/MI traffic to the debug console for diagnostics.

program.description: Path to the host C++ executable currently running the Nautilus IR.

irFile.description: Optional Nautilus IR file currently of interest (used to add a source-search directory).

initialConfigurations.name: Debug Nautilus IR via host program (gdb)

configurationSnippets.label: Nautilus IR: Launch host program with gdb
configurationSnippets.description: Debug a Nautilus IR file by launching the C++ host program that loads it under gdb.

body.name: Debug Nautilus IR via host program (gdb)

configurationSnippets.label: Nautilus IR: Attach to host process with gdb
configurationSnippets.description: Attach to a running C++ host process executing Nautilus IR.
